rules: Never store credentials, tokens, or passwords in plaintext. Always hash passwords with bcrypt (cost >= 12). Never log auth material.
Rotate secrets via environment variables only — never hardcode them in source files.
Never DROP COLUMN or DROP TABLE. Only ADD COLUMN with a safe default. Never run destructive DDL without an explicit rollback script alongside it.
All schema changes must be wrapped in a transaction. If the transaction cannot roll back the change, do not proceed.
Never modify production source files. Write tests only. Do not fix the code under test — report the failure instead.
Every test assertion must include a failure message that identifies which acceptance criterion it covers.
Validate and sanitize all inputs at the API boundary. Never pass raw user input to a database query or shell command.
Return structured error responses (status code + message). Never expose stack traces or internal paths in API responses.
